explanation: The requirement specifies that when a 'change mode' command is received from the GUI (i.e., PSVModeSelected), the mode should transition from 'VentilationOff' (represented in the provided atomic propositions as StandbyMode) to 'PSV' (represented as PSVMode). This is a cause-effect relationship where the reception of PSVModeSelected (the cause) should trigger the system to enter PSVMode (the effect). The template chosen encodes that whenever PSVModeSelected is true, PSVMode must immediately hold in the next time step. Bool_exp2 is set to PSVModeSelected (the trigger condition), and bool_exp3 is set to PSVMode (the target/desired effect), capturing the transition logic as described in the requirement.

The property expresses that whenever the PSVModeSelected event occurs (meaning the user has initiated a mode change to PSV from the GUI), the system must transition out of VentilationOff and into PSV. In terms of atomic propositions, we use StandbyMode to represent "VentilationOff" and PSVMode for "PSV", and PSVModeSelected as the event triggered by the GUI. Because LTL cannot directly express state transitions, we formally say that whenever PSVModeSelected becomes true, in the next moment (X), it should not be StandbyMode anymore, but should be PSVMode; that is, a transition from StandbyMode to PSVMode is expected when PSVModeSelected is triggered. Therefore, we use the formula: G (PSVModeSelected -> (X (!StandbyMode & PSVMode))). This ensures that every time the change mode command to PSV is issued from the GUI, the system will perform the transition from StandbyMode (aka VentilationOff) to PSV in the next state.

The natural language specifies a state transition: from VentilationOff (StandbyMode) to PSV (PSVMode) should occur if the change mode command is received from the GUI. The atomic proposition 'PSVModeSelected' best matches 'change mode command ... received from the GUI' (specifically, to PSV mode), and 'PSVMode' indicates the active state after transition. The corresponding LTL property should state that whenever PSVModeSelected is true (the mode change command is received), then in the next step the PSVMode becomes active (the transition occurs). Therefore, we choose template 11: G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3)), where bool_exp2 is PSVModeSelected and bool_exp3 is PSVMode.

The property expresses that whenever the PSVModeSelected atomic proposition ("indicates switch to PSV mode initiated by the user") is true in the current step (i.e., the change mode command to enter PSV is received from the GUI), the system should transition from VentilationOff (not PSVMode) to PSV (PSVMode). Since we only have atomic propositions including PSVMode, and StandbyMode = VentilationOff, we capture the transition by stating: at every point, if PSVModeSelected becomes true, then in the next state, the system should be in PSVMode (i.e., PSV is active after selecting it from off). This guarantees that whenever the mode change is triggered in Standby (VentilationOff) mode, the transition to PSV occurs immediately.

The requirement states that the system should transition from VentilationOff (StandbyMode) to PSV upon receipt of the change mode command from the GUI (PSVModeSelected). In terms of atomic propositions, this means: whenever PSVModeSelected becomes true (received from GUI), then system must transition to Pressure Support Ventilation mode (PSVMode, i.e., controller should be in PSV). This can be captured with 'G (PSVModeSelected -> PSVMode)', which says: always, if PSVModeSelected is true at any time step, then PSVMode will (immediately or eventually) be true (typically, in LTL, mode transition is modelled by PSVMode becoming true after/unless more implementation details say otherwise). This uses template 30.

The natural language requirement states that the system may switch from 'VentilationOff' (defined here as 'StandbyMode') to 'PSV' mode (where 'PSVMode' is true) if the user issues a change mode command from the GUI (represented by 'PSVModeSelected'). This corresponds to a transition logic: whenever the system is in Standby (ventilation off), if the user requests Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) mode via the GUI, the system should make the transition so that 'PSVMode' eventually holds. This is best represented by a property that, globally, whenever the precondition (StandbyMode & PSVModeSelected) holds, 'PSVMode' becomes true in the next state. Thus, template 41 maps perfectly: G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3)), with 'StandbyMode', 'PSVModeSelected', and 'PSVMode' filled in respectively.

The input states that the transition from VentilationOff (StandbyMode) to PSV (PSVMode) should occur when the user issues the command to change to PSV from the GUI (PSVModeSelected). This means, whenever 'PSVModeSelected' is true, then in the next step, 'PSVMode' should become true (i.e., the mode switches to PSV in the following state after the command). This property is captured by the template: G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3)), where bool_exp2 is 'PSVModeSelected' (the command to switch mode) and bool_exp3 is 'PSVMode' (now in PSV mode). Thus, the LTL property expresses that whenever the selection command for PSV is received, the mode will transition to PSV in the next step, modeling the requested behavior.

The property requires that whenever the PSVModeSelected signal is received (indicating that the user, via the GUI, has issued a command to switch to PSV mode), the system must transition to PSV mode. In this context, 'PSVModeSelected' represents the reception of the change mode command, and 'PSVMode' indicates that the ventilator is in Pressure Support Ventilation Mode (i.e., the desired state after the transition). The selected LTL template expresses that globally, whenever the PSVModeSelected event occurs, eventually PSVMode will become true, thus encoding the required transition from VentilationOff to PSV mode upon command.

The natural language requirement describes that the state transition from "VentilationOff" (which aligns with StandbyMode in the atomic proposition list) to "PSV" mode should only occur if the "change mode command" to Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) is received from the GUI. The closest atomic proposition that captures the user command for switching to PSV is PSVModeSelected. Therefore, we want to specify that whenever PSVModeSelected is true (i.e., the user selected PSV mode), then in the next step, the system should enter PSVMode (Pressure Support Ventilation is active). This ensures the transition is only triggered as specified. Thus, we use template 11: G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3)), with bool_exp2 = PSVModeSelected and bool_exp3 = PSVMode.

This LTL property reads: "Globally, if the change mode command for PSV is received from the GUI (PSVModeSelected), then in the next step the system shall be in PSV operating mode (PSVMode)." This captures exactly the requested semantics for the transition.

The natural language requirement states that the transition from VentilationOff (i.e., StandbyMode) to PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation Mode) should occur if the change mode command is received from the GUI. The atomic proposition 'PSVModeSelected' represents the event when a switch to PSV mode is initiated by the user (received from the GUI). The best LTL template is 'G (bool_exp2 -> X bool_exp3)', where bool_exp2 corresponds to 'StandbyMode' (indicating VentilationOff), and bool_exp3 corresponds to 'PSVModeSelected' (indicates switch to PSV mode initiated by the user). This LTL formula means: Whenever the system is in VentilationOff (StandbyMode) and 'PSVModeSelected' is received from GUI, then in the next step the transition to PSV mode should occur. N_DURATION is not required for this template.
